title: Endpoint, Mobile, Browser, Wireless, and SOHO Hardening · Causal Recall
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs endpoint, mobile, browser, wireless, and soho hardening from user impact, asset identity, safety, baseline, symptom, recent change, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, verification, prevention, escalation, documentation, and transfer evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for layered endpoint hardening and exception register after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Inventory exposed services, software, accounts, privileges, defaults, updates, encryption, logs, backups, recovery, accessibility, owner, and exception before hardening.
Choose supported personal or enterprise wireless authentication, encryption, credentials, certificates, onboarding, rotation, revocation, and guest isolation.
Apply screen lock, supported updates, encryption, secure enrollment, app sources, permissions, backups, remote response, connectivity controls, and privacy-aware management.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Hardening Baseline · changed case · Review Hardening Baseline
steps.instruction:
review case — Browsers, extensions, mobile apps, personal devices, home routers, and public Wi-Fi use defaults with inconsistent updates and recovery. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test original user tasks, assistive technology, remote and offline access, updates, recovery, logs, exceptions, changed identities, and hostile cases after hardening.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Record what required effort · Review Hardening Regression, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Hardening Regression precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Record what required effort · Review Hardening Regression, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Browsers, extensions, mobile apps, personal devices, home routers, and public Wi-Fi use defaults with inconsistent updates and recovery. The review variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Test original user tasks, assistive technology, remote and offline access, updates, recovery, logs, exceptions, changed identities, and hostile cases after hardening.

For CompTIA A+ support Record what required effort · Review Hardening Regression, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: A stricter setting is always safer even when it breaks recovery or creates an insecure workaround.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using test and original plus specific case evidence.
